item.text: I’ll identify all direct `run_deterministic(...)` call sites under `crates/orbit-engine/src/`, then map each call to its containing `fn` and filter out test/example paths. I’m starting with a code search to collect candidate locations, then I’ll open each file to verify function names precisely.
I found 5 candidate `.run_deterministic(` call sites in `dispatcher.rs` and `groundhog.rs`. Next I’m verifying each line’s enclosing function so we only return exact production call sites.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "for f in crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/dispatcher.rs crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/groundhog.rs; do awk 'match("'$0,/''^[[:space:]]*fn[[:space:]]+([A-Za-z0-9_]+)/,m){fn=m[1]} /'"\\.run_deterministic[[:space:]]*\\(/ {printf \"%s:%d:%s\\n\", FILENAME, FNR, fn}' \""'$f"; done'

/bin/zsh -lc "for f in crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/dispatcher.rs crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/groundhog.rs; do awk 'match("'$0,/''^[[:space:]]*fn[[:space:]]+([A-Za-z0-9_]+)/,m){fn=m[1]} /'"\\.run_deterministic[[:space:]]*\\(/ {printf \"%s:%d:%s\\n\", FILENAME, FNR, fn}' \""'$f"; done'

item.text: I hit a small `awk` syntax issue while trying to auto-map call lines to enclosing `fn`; I’m re-running that verification with a simpler parser command.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "for f in crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/dispatcher.rs crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/groundhog.rs; do perl -ne 'if(/"'^'"\\s*fn\\s+([A-Za-z0-9_]+)/){"'$fn=$1} if(/'"\\.run_deterministic\\s*\\(/){print \""'$ARGV:$.:$fn'"\\n\"}' \""'$f"; done'

/bin/zsh -lc "for f in crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/dispatcher.rs crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/groundhog.rs; do perl -ne 'if(/"'^'"\\s*fn\\s+([A-Za-z0-9_]+)/){"'$fn=$1} if(/'"\\.run_deterministic\\s*\\(/){print \""'$ARGV:$.:$fn'"\\n\"}' \""'$f"; done'
